source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A sequence \( x(n) \) has non-zero values as shown in the <image>.
The sequence
$$
y(n)=\left\{\begin{array}{ll}
x\left(\frac{n}{2}-1\right), & \text{for } n \text{ is even} \\
0, & \text{for } n \text{ is odd}
\end{array}\right.
$$
will be:
extraction_notes: solution field contains only option letter; mapped to corresponding choice text
image field is a PIL image object representation, not a path or URL

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the non-zero values are 'shown in the image'
all answer choices are diagram-based ('diagram A' to 'diagram D'), so the visual content is central
multi_step: solving requires reading the original sequence x(n) from the image and then applying the transformation y(n)=x(n/2-1) for even n
the definition also imposes a second condition that y(n)=0 for odd n
the task is to map the transformed sequence to one of four diagram choices, which involves combining the formula with the visual sequence pattern
verifiability: answer is constrained to a small fixed set of choices (4 diagrams), which makes final-answer checking straightforward
answer_present=true, answer_type='short_text', and the provided answer matches one listed choice text
registry_passed=true with no recorded issues, so the sample assets and text are available for downstream checking

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present, so the core semantics are recoverable
asset registry passed with no recorded issues, indicating good asset completeness
initial scoring is strong overall, with high image dependency, good multi-step reasoning value, and high verifiability
